items.title: Sources: Fulham bid $38m for USMNT's Pepi
items.summary: Fulham have made a bid of €32 million ($38m) for the services of PSV forward Ricardo Pepi, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Estêvão on scoresheet as Chelsea brush Palace aside
items.summary: Estêvão opened the scoring and added an assist as Chelsea secured a 3-1 victory over 10-man Crystal Palace.

items.title: Aston Villa dream alive after beating Newcastle
items.summary: Emi Buendia's sweet strike set Aston Villa on their way to a hard-fought 2-0 Premier League win at Newcastle.

items.title: Van Dijk: No problem with Liverpool 'togetherness'
items.summary: Virgil van Dijk believes Liverpool lack consistency but not togetherness after the club's hopes of Champions League qualification were dealt a blow.

items.title: Almada strikes as Atlético cruise past Mallorca
items.summary: Atlético Madrid earned back-to-back LaLiga wins with a 3-0 victory over Mallorca.

items.title: Utrecht-opponent Celtic geeft zege uit handen in beladen Schotse topper
items.summary: De Schotse topper tussen Hearts en Celtic is in balans gebleven. De wedstrijd eindigde in een 2-2 gelijkspel. Celtic neemt het donderdag op tegen FC Utrecht in de Europa League.

items.title: Arsenal v Manchester United: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Updates from 4.30pm GMT fixture at Emirates Stadium ⚽ Jonathan Wilson: is Carrick more than the new Solskjær? ⚽ Scores | Tables | Premier League top scorers This, from my MBM colleague Tim de Lisle, is a terrific read on what Michael Carrick’s Manchester United did so well last weekend. United showed more intensity than usual, as you’d expect in a derby, but they also showed more composure. Carrick had called for it beforehand, and as possibly the calmest player ever seen in a United shirt, he speaks with some authority on the subject. When Amad broke away in the 33rd minute, he coolly rounded Donnarumma and rolled the ball into an empty net. When Fernandes broke away eight minutes later, he went one better, landing a defender on his backside after rounding the keeper. Both goals were disallowed, but they stood as statements of intent. It’s never the same again! Every game is different but we’ve had a good week and we’re in good shape. We understand this has been a tough place to come for many teams. But we’re going into the game in a good spirit and that’s the most important thing. Continue reading...

items.title: Barça blijft koploper na stiftje Raphinha en prachtgoal Lamine Yamal
items.summary: Barcelona heeft in eigen huis tegen Real Oviedo geen fout gemaakt. Met aanvoerder Frenkie de Jong had de thuisploeg het slechts één helft lastig tegen de hekkensluiter van La Liga.

items.title: VI Live: uitvallen Watanabe zure appel voor Feyenoord in moeizame tweede helft
items.summary: Feyenoord en Heracles Almelo staan zondagmiddag om 16.45 uur tegenover elkaar in de Eredivisie. De Rotterdammers verspeelden vier competitieduels op rij punten en azen dus op eerherstel. Dat kan tegen de hekkensluiter van Nederland. Volg het duel in de VI Live.

items.title: VI Live: United op gelijke hoogte bij Arsenal na enorme fout Zubimendi
items.summary: Zondag wordt er weer volop gevoetbald in binnen- en buitenland. AZ gaat om 12.15 uur op bezoek bij Telstar, FC Groningen speelt tegen Fortuna Sittard, FC Utrecht ontvangt Sparta Rotterdam en ook in het buitenland staat ons veel moois te wachten. Volg het in de VI Live.

items.title: Ünüvar verruilt Enschede voor Almelo
items.summary: Naci Ünüvar (22) verlaat FC Twente op huurbasis en maakt het seizoen af bij Heracles Almelo. De aanvaller bevond zich op een zijspoor in De Grolsch Veste, waar hij zaterdagavond de hele wedstrijd tegen Excelsior op de bank bleef.

items.title: Leicester sack Cifuentes after six months as boss
items.summary: Marti Cifuentes is sacked as Leicester City manager after just six months in charge with the Foxes in the bottom half of the Championship.

items.title: Man City 'got job done' - so is WSL title race over?
items.summary: Manchester City capitalised on Chelsea's defeat to open up a nine-point lead at the top of the Women's Super League. Is the title race over?

items.title: Fernández helps Chelsea into top four as Palace pay penalty before Wharton red
items.summary: If this was a taste of what life on the road under Liam Rosenior might be like for Chelsea fans then anyone who doubted his appointment may want to think again. A stunning individual performance from Estêvão, who scored the first after a mistake from fellow teenager Jaydee Canvot before setting up João Pedro for the second, inspired his side to end a run of five Premier League games without an away victory at the first attempt as they piled on the misery for Crystal Palace, who also had Adam Wharton sent off. A controversial penalty from Enzo Fernández compounded a terrible afternoon for Canvot – the 19-year-old tasked with replacing captain Marc Guéhi after he was sold to Manchester City – as Oliver Glasner’s side stretched their winless run against Chelsea in the Premier League to 17 matches. It is now also 11 games without a victory in all competitions for last season’s FA Cup winners despite a late consolation from Chris Richards, with Palace nervously looking over their shoulders and wondering where the next points are coming from with trips to Nottingham Forest and arch-rivals Brighton up next. Continue reading...

items.title: Igor Jesus and Awoniyi sink Brentford to ease Forest away from relegation danger
items.summary: Nottingham Forest finished their rollercoaster week on the up, with three away points against Brentford restoring their five-point buffer from the relegation zone. Mouthwatering goals from Forest’s maligned strikers, first Igor Jesus with a blaster then a solo effort from Taiwo Awoniyi, put memories of fan displeasure after defeat in the Europa League behind Sean Dyche and his team and confirmed four league points in eight days after their Arsenal draw. Awoniyi’s goal in particular was a welcome milestone, his first since being placed in an induced coma after he collided with a post at the end of last season. Continue reading...

items.title: Chelsea beat 10-man Palace at Selhurst Park to move fourth
items.summary: FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Crystal Palace’s match against Chelsea in the Premier League.

items.title: Buendía and Watkins stun Newcastle to keep Aston Villa’s title hopes alive
items.summary: Some wins are a little bit lucky and others downright fluky or simply ground out, but this Aston Villa victory belonged to a different category. It was the sort of triumph that can be filed under “thoroughly deserved”. Indeed, much of Villa’s attacking play was so fluid, fluent and gloriously improvisational that, by comparison, Newcastle looked as if they were engaged in a footballing equivalent of painting by numbers. Continue reading...

items.title: Forest claim crucial win to move FIVE clear of relegation zone
items.summary: FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Brentford’s match against Nottingham Forest in the Premier League.

items.title: Forest stave off West Ham resurgence with crucial win at Brentford
items.summary: Goals in either half from Igor Jesus and Taiwo Awoniyi saw Nottingham Forest restore their five-point gap above resurgent West Ham and the Premier League relegation zone with a potentially vital 2-0 win at Brentford.

items.title: Chelsea win convincingly at Palace to move up to fourth
items.summary: Chelsea moved into the Champions League places with a 3-1 win over out-of-form Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park.
